meaning: vt.尽(力),施加(压力等),努力v.发挥,竭尽全力,尽
examples: A number of neurotransmitters exert their effects by first increasing cyclic AMP concentrations in target neurons.
In order to be successful he would have to exert himself.
We all know that when we exert ourselves our heart rate increases.
But the authors emphasize that city policymakers do exert their influence, with tangible results.
Until the deal is inked, however America may exert its best efforts behind the scenes.
